title: The unit of evaluation is now the workflow
summary: Benchmark scores are becoming weaker summaries of useful capability in agentic settings.
What is being evaluated now is often a composite workflow, not just a model.
critique: Single-number comparisons hide differences in harness design, reliability logic, and budget constraints.
Systems that look similar on benchmarks may diverge sharply in long-horizon practical use.
whoShouldCare: Anyone using benchmarks to compare agent systems, evaluate open-vs-closed gaps, or reason about practical deployment quality.
openQuestions: What evaluation formats best expose workflow robustness rather than benchmark theater?
How should cost, recoverability, and inspectability be represented in agent evaluations?
hotTake: In agentic work, benchmark parity can coexist with workflow inequality.
